div.h3: Why Previous-Term Entries Cannot Be Directly Committed

p.em: Raft Paper: "Leader only advances commitIndex for entries from its current term"

div.p: Context: Term 2 Replication S1 is leader in Term 2. It replicates entry at index 2 to S2. Current state: 2/5 nodes have entry. Status: UNCOMMITTED (Minority)
Crash & Loss of Leadership S1 crashes before committing index 2. The entry {T2, 2} is volatile. Remaining cluster: S2, S3, S4, S5.
Election Dynamics S5 wins election for Term 3 (with votes from S3, S4). S5 accepts a new command at index 2. Conflict: S5 has {T3, 2}, S2 has {T2, 2}.
Safety Result S5 replicates its current-term entry {T3, 2}. Followers (S2, S3, S4) must overwrite. If S1 had told a client {T2, 2} was committed... WE WOULD HAVE A DATA LOSS BUG.
A leader cannot conclude that an entry from a previous term is committed simply by counting replicas.
It must reach a majority with an entry from its current term.

ol.li: Entry is from current term? → Majority = Committed.
Entry is from older term? → WAIT until a later current-term entry commits.

div.p: Transitive Property: When {T3, idx3} commits, all prior entries (including T2 at idx2) are automatically committed and sealed.
